summary: Backend smoke + endpoint testing for Pelangi Homestay. Auth, /rooms, /products, /reports/summary, /reports/daily, /audit-log, role-based 403, overbooking 400, room status PUT all work. CRITICAL: POST /checkins, POST /kasir, POST /expenses all return 500 Internal Server Error due to ObjectId serialization bug (the inserted doc returned after Motor's insert_one() contains _id ObjectId which FastAPI cannot encode). This breaks the entire checkin -> checkout -> housekeeping flow and the cashier/expenses creation. Downstream tests (preview/checkout/housekeeping-done/insufficient-payment) could not be executed because /checkins fails.
passed: POST /checkins overbooking on non-kosong room -> 400 'Kamar belum tersedia...'
PUT /rooms/{id}/status menginap OK, day_use rejected 400, kosong OK
Resepsionis blocked from POST /users, GET /users, POST /rooms (403)
GET /reports/summary returns expected structure (rooms breakdown, pendapatan, etc.)

failed.issue: Returns 500 Internal Server Error after successfully inserting checkin. Backend logs show: ValueError: [TypeError("'ObjectId' object is not iterable"), TypeError('vars() argument must have __dict__ attribute')] from fastapi/encoders.py serialize_response. Root cause: server.py:443 `await db.checkins.insert_one(doc)` mutates `doc` by adding `_id` ObjectId, then line 446 `return doc` triggers JSON encoding of ObjectId which fails. Room status DOES change to day_use (side-effect succeeds), but client gets 500.
failed.evidence: POST /api/checkins HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error; traceback 'ObjectId is not iterable' in /var/log/supervisor/backend.err.log

failed.issue: Same ObjectId serialization bug. server.py:639 inserts then line 645 `return doc` fails. Transaction is created in DB but client receives 500. Blocks UI receipt display.
failed.evidence: POST /api/kasir HTTP/1.1 500 Internal Server Error (reproduced with both laundry and non-laundry products, valid payment)

failed.issue: Same ObjectId serialization bug at server.py:672-674. Insert succeeds (visible via GET /expenses) but POST response is 500.
failed.evidence: POST /api/expenses (with correct {kategori, deskripsi, nominal}) -> 500 Internal Server Error

failed.issue: Could not test GET /checkins/{id} preview, POST /checkins/{id}/checkout (success + insufficient payment), POST /rooms/{id}/housekeeping-done because /checkins POST returns 500 to the client (cannot capture checkin_id reliably). housekeeping-done also could not be exercised since no room reached perlu_dibersihkan status.
failed.evidence: Dependency on POST /checkins response which returns 500

action_items: Fix POST /checkins, /kasir, /expenses (and audit any other endpoints that `return doc` after `insert_one`). Standard fix: either pop '_id' from doc before return (`doc.pop('_id', None)`), or build a clean response dict, or use a Pydantic response_model that excludes _id. Same pattern likely affects any other POST that returns the inserted doc.
After fix, re-test full flow: checkin -> GET preview -> insufficient-payment 400 -> checkout success -> room=perlu_dibersihkan -> housekeeping-done -> room=kosong.
Verify kasir stock decrement for makanan/minuman and not for laundry after fix.
Verify expense appears in GET /expenses after fix (already confirmed insert side-effect works).
